channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Key Political, Economic and Sports Developments Across Nigeria and Abroad on 31st May 2026
item.description: Today’s Nigerian newspaper roundup captures major developments across politics, economy, security, sports, and entertainment. In the economy, Dangote Petroleum Refinery announced a reduction in petrol...

item.title: Woman dies in early-morning collision in Birmingham city centre, man arrested
item.description: A 28-year-old woman tragically lost her life after being struck by a black Audi RSQ8 on Dartmouth Middleway in Birmingham city centre shortly before 2:30am. The incident led to the closure of the A454...

item.title: Trump could maintain influence over the Republican Party without seeking another term
item.description: A Republican strategist, Liam Donavan, shared his perspective on former President Donald Trump's ongoing influence within the GOP, emphasizing that Trump may not pursue a third term but could still ex...

item.title: Regina symposium focuses on youth education around addiction, recovery, and mental health
item.description: A community event in Regina brought together youth, educators, and health professionals to raise awareness about addiction, recovery, and mental health. The initiative aimed to provide young people wi...

item.title: US-Iran Peace Talks Show Progress, But Tensions Remain
item.description: In Singapore, US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth indicated that while negotiations with Iran over ending the recent conflict have been productive, the possibility of military action remains on the tabl...

item.title: Climate Activists Protest TotalEnergies Over Fossil Fuel Projects in Africa
item.description: On 29 May 2026, climate activists gathered outside TotalEnergies’ Johannesburg offices to protest the company’s fossil fuel projects in East Africa, Mozambique, Namibia, and South Africa. The demonstr...

item.title: Kerry-Lynne Findlay elected leader of BC Conservatives ahead of next provincial election
item.description: Kerry-Lynne Findlay, a lawyer and former federal minister of national revenue under former prime minister Stephen Harper, has been elected leader of the British Columbia Conservative Party. She secure...

item.title: Lerena loses WBC bridgerweight crown to Merhy in Charleroi rematch
item.description: Kevin Lerena’s bid to defend his WBC bridgerweight title ended in defeat as Ryad Merhy outboxed him over 12 rounds in Charleroi, Belgium. The judges’ scores—116-112, 117-111, and 115-113—reflected Mer...

item.title: Apple Plans Significant Camera Upgrades for Future iPhones, Including Variable Aperture and Ultra-Wide Module Changes
item.description: Apple is planning major upgrades to its iPhone camera systems over the next few years, according to analyst Ming-Chi Kuo. For the upcoming iPhone 18 Pro, the most notable change will be the introducti...

item.title: B.C. Conservative Party to announce leadership vote following leadership change
item.description: The B.C. Conservative Party is set to announce details regarding a leadership vote following the recent ouster of its previous leader, John Rustad. The development marks a significant moment for the p...

item.title: Ramaphosa Reviews Madlanga Commission Report to Address Issues in Criminal Justice System
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa is currently studying the second interim report of the Madlanga Commission, describing the process as crucial in tackling alleged misconduct within South Africa’s criminal ju...

item.title: Kerry-Lynne Findlay chosen as leader of the British Columbia Conservative Party
item.description: Former Member of Parliament Kerry-Lynne Findlay has been elected as the new leader of the British Columbia Conservative Party, according to a report published on May 30, 2026. The announcement marks a...

item.title: MK Party Chief Whip Faces Multiple Fraud and Corruption Allegations
item.description: Mmabatho Mokoena-Zondi, the Chief Whip of the uMkhonto weSizwe Party (MKP), is embroiled in serious fraud and corruption allegations. Recently granted R30,000 bail, she faces charges for allegedly ded...

item.title: Heated Rivalry leads Canadian Screen Awards with 13 wins, alongside strong showings from North of North
item.description: The Crave drama series Heated Rivalry emerged as the biggest winner at the Canadian Screen Awards scripted television ceremony, securing 13 awards and firmly establishing itself as one of the most cel...

item.title: Socceroos face Mexico in World Cup warm-up at the Rose Bowl in Pasadena
item.description: The Socceroos have begun their final preparations for the upcoming World Cup campaign with a high-profile warm-up match against Mexico at the iconic Rose Bowl in Pasadena, California. The live blog ca...

item.title: Trump considers replacing America 250 concert with political rally after performer withdrawals
item.description: President Donald Trump suggested replacing a planned America 250 anniversary concert with a large political rally after several scheduled performers withdrew from the event. The concert, part of the b...

item.title: Trump to Lead US 250th Anniversary Celebration After Performers Withdraw
item.description: Several artists originally scheduled to perform at the opening concerts for the Great American State Fair, part of the United States' 250th birthday celebrations, have withdrawn citing concerns over t...

item.title: Philippines assesses its competitiveness in EV and AI manufacturing amid rising ASEAN industrial competition
item.description: The article discusses the growing competition among Southeast Asian countries as the ASEAN manufacturing sector continues to accelerate, particularly in emerging industries such as electric vehicles (...

item.title: Edmundo González urges fresh presidential elections in Venezuela amid interim government
item.description: Former Venezuelan opposition candidate Edmundo González has called for the holding of fresh presidential elections in Venezuela as the country approaches the five-month mark of interim President Delcy...

item.title: Broos' challenging tenure culminates in South Africa returning to the World Cup stage
item.description: Bareng-Batho Kortjaas, writing for the Sunday Times, reflects on Hugo Broos’ controversial yet transformative tenure as South Africa’s Bafana Bafana coach. The piece portrays Broos as a polarising fig...
